sections.paragraph:
ἀγαθοποιΐα, ας, ἡ (Ptolem., Apotel. 1, 18, 4 ed. FBoll-EBoer ’40; Vett. Val. 164, 17; Vi. Aesopi III p. 309, 8) engagement in doing what is good, doing good (TestJos 18:2 v.l. B-D-F §119, 1); together with other ἀγαθ-terms, ἀ. is part of the semantic field relating to the esteem in which Gr-Rom. persons of exceptional merit were held. κτίστῃ παρατίθεσθαι τὰς ψυχὰς ἐν ἀγαθοποιΐᾳ (v.l. ἀγαθοποιΐαις) entrust their souls to the creator while (or by) doing good, which can be taken gener. or as meaning specif. acts (so, if pl.) 1 Pt 4:19; πόθος εἰς ἀ. a longing to do good 1 Cl 2:2—cp. 2:7; 33:1; πρόθυμος εἰς ἀ. eager to do good 34:2.—TW. Spicq. Sv.

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Vi. Aesopi Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

gener. gener. = generally

specif. specif. = specifically

  1. pl. = plural

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 3
